sections.title: Part 1: Sentence Structure (The W-Question Rule)
sections.description: In German, when you ask a question using a question word (like Who, What, Where), the structure is very strict. This is often called the V2 Rule because the verb must always be in the second position.

content.key_rule: Key Rule: The question word always starts with the letter "w" and sits at Position 1. The conjugated verb follows immediately at Position 2.

sections.title: Part 2: Common W-Words and Their Usage
sections.description: Here is a breakdown of the most common question words, how to use them, and why they are chosen.

categories.description: German changes the word for "who" depending on the grammatical case (Subject, Direct Object, or Indirect Object).

rows: Used when the person is the receiver of an action
Wen suchst du? (Whom are you looking for?)
Mit wem gehst du? (With whom are you going?)

categories.description: German distinguishes between being stationary, coming from somewhere, or moving toward a destination.

rows: Wo wohnen Sie? (Where do you live?)
Asks about where someone/something comes from
Woher kommen Sie? (Where are you from?)
Asks about the destination or where someone is going
Wohin gehen Sie? (Where are you going to?)

categories.description: Used for objects, things, or activities.

examples.question: Was sind Sie von Beruf? (What is your profession?)

categories.description: Used for names, descriptions, or characteristics.

examples.question: Wie heißen Sie? (What/How are you named?)
Wie alt ist er? (How old is he?)

sections.title: Part 3: Advanced W-Words
sections.description: More complex question words for specific contexts.

rows: Asks for a reason. The answer usually starts with Weil (because).
Warum kommst du spät? (Why are you coming late?)
Wann beginnt der Film? (When does the film start?)
Used for uncountable things or prices.
Wie viel kostet das? (How much does that cost?)
Wie viele Kinder haben Sie? (How many children do you have?)

sections.title: Summary Table for Students
sections.description: Quick reference guide for all W-question words:

rows: Use for the subject (the person doing the action).
Use for a fixed location (no movement).
Use with verbs of movement like "gehen" or "fahren".
Use for names, age, or "How is...".
Use to ask for a reason or cause.
